summary:
Claim: Gamma-carboxylation of glutamate does not require biotin as a cofactor.  
Evidence: - Gamma-carboxylation depends exclusively on vitamin K for post-translational modification of clotting factors. - All other listed conversions (acetyl-CoA to malonyl-CoA, pyruvate to oxaloacetate, propionyl-CoA to methylmalonyl-CoA) are catalyzed by biotin-dependent carboxylases.  
Answer: A  

(498 tokens)
